focus: Cross-cutting consolidation, dedupe, severity recalibration, coverage map, interaction scan, and adversarial validation of R5 findings

reason: Same collector-level root cause: caller-provided metric labels are accepted without declared label-policy enforcement. R4 contributes skill_id evidence to the retained R1 metrics-cardinality finding.
R1-P2-001 is downstream-of the PR-3 deletion-sweep mismatch in R1-P1-002; stale manual evidence should be folded into the retained delete-inventory traceability finding.

The env leak is real, but no existing default-suite failure was shown, the focused run passed, and vitest.config.ts serializes files. It is order-sensitive test hygiene rather than a demonstrated merge blocker.

evidence: vitest.config.ts includes mcp_server/skill-advisor/bench/**/*.bench.ts; package.json test:core runs plain vitest; code-graph-parse-latency.bench.ts requires dist/startup-checks.js; git ls-files returned no tracked dist/startup-checks.js.
All three bench files set SPECKIT_METRICS_ENABLED=true and do not restore the previous env value, but this pass found no current regular test failure caused by the leak.

hook-brief-signal-noise.bench.ts constructs only positive live renderables, asserts rendered-string presence, and manually increments recommendation_emitted_total instead of driving production metric emission.

All PRs 1-10 have at least one reviewed file across iterations 1-5. Under-reviewed surfaces remain PR-1 scorer-bench.ts, PR-6 cache invalidation lifecycle/concurrency, PR-10 production renderer negatives, generated dist hook parity, and root/workspace script orchestration.
